behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "wait time between retries doubles... up to a configured maximum" is a pure backoff rule. Real failure boundary is local math/state progression in retry-delay calculation, with no required module handoff, OS, network, subprocess, or model output. Rejection gate: Integration/System were considered, but disqualified because this behavior text does not require request execution or external I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Test the delay-calculation function/object directly with consecutive failure counts (e.g., 1..N) and assert exponential growth with cap at configured max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "identifies a rate-limit response and computes... wait time" requires response classification + retry policy wiring, a module-boundary handoff. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; stubbed responses are enough, so not System. (b) no AI/ML output, so not Agentic. (c) no multi-agent chain, so not Workflow. Rejection gate: Unit was rejected because a pure function test would miss classifier→backoff integration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire client retry component with a stub transport returning a 429-like response (with/without retry headers), then assert computed next-delay is correct for each case.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "after the wait... resends the original request and returns the successful result" is retry-loop orchestration across timer/scheduler + transport + result propagation. Integration brake: no need for real HTTP or OS timers if using in-process stub transport and fake clock; therefore not System. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub transport that fails with rate-limit once then succeeds; assert same request payload is resent after scheduled delay and final resolved value matches success response.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "after the configured maximum number of retries" + "returns a typed QuotaExhaustedError" depends on retry counter termination plus error mapping at the client boundary. Integration brake: no real OS/network needed to expose this; deterministic stubs suffice. Rejection gate: Unit considered but disqualified because counting retries and surfacing typed error typically spans retry engine + public API boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries, stub transport to always return rate-limit, advance fake time through attempts, and assert the final rejected error is `QuotaExhaustedError` (and not generic).

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "full retry chain... quota tracking... terminates correctly" is cross-module control-flow safety, not a single-function property. Integration brake: (a) can be reproduced with in-process components and fake clock, so System not required. (b/c) no AI or multi-agent steps. Rejection gate: System considered for "does not loop indefinitely," but behavior text does not require real process/network conditions; internal loop termination can fail in pure in-process orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real in-process retry pipeline with always-rate-limited stub transport and shared quota tracker, advance fake timers, assert bounded attempt count and eventual typed terminal error within expected virtual time.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "multiple async callers in the same process" and "same shared quota bucket" is a shared-state/concurrency interaction across caller-facing API + quota manager modules. Integration brake: same-process async concurrency does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process concurrency test can catch real race/state bugs. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior explicitly scopes to same process and does not require external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Fire concurrent `call()` invocations against a stubbed transport while observing a real shared quota bucket instance; assert aggregate consumption is global (not per-caller) and throttling decisions reflect combined demand.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote gate: "multiple requests are waiting" and "dispatched in the order... submitted" requires queueing + scheduler + dispatcher interaction; this is a module-boundary ordering guarantee. Integration brake: no real network/OS required to observe FIFO violations; in-process fake clock and stub transport are sufficient. Rejection gate: Unit rejected because isolated queue tests can miss ordering regressions introduced by scheduler/dispatcher wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue several calls under throttled conditions, control time progression, and assert dispatch timestamps/order match submission order end-to-end through the client pipeline.
